title: Severe Bleeding
source: American Red Cross / Stop the Bleed

slides.title: Call 911
slides.content: Call emergency services immediately. Tell them your location and that there is severe uncontrolled bleeding.
slides.warning: Do not delay applying pressure while waiting for help.
slides.tts_text: Call 911 immediately. Tell them your location and that there is severe bleeding.

slides.title: Apply Direct Pressure
slides.content: Place a clean cloth, gauze, or bandage directly on the wound. Press firmly with both hands. Maintain constant pressure for at least 15 minutes.
slides.warning: If the cloth soaks through, add more on top — do NOT remove the original dressing. Removing it can disrupt the clot.
slides.tts_text: Place a clean cloth on the wound and press firmly. Do not lift the cloth. Add more material on top if it soaks through.

slides.title: Is the wound on an arm or leg?
slides.content: Location determines whether a tourniquet is an option.
slides.tts_text: Is the wound on an arm or a leg?

slides.title: Apply a Tourniquet (Limb Wounds)
slides.content: If bleeding will not stop with direct pressure after 5 minutes: Apply tourniquet 2–3 inches above the wound. Never place over a joint. Tighten until bleeding stops. Write the time applied on the person's skin.
slides.warning: A tourniquet is correct for limb bleeds that won't stop. Do not remove it once applied — only EMS should remove it.
slides.tts_text: Apply tourniquet 2 to 3 inches above the wound. Tighten until bleeding stops. Write the time on the skin.

slides.title: Torso / Neck / Groin Bleeding
slides.content: Maintain firm direct pressure continuously. For deep groin or armpit wounds: pack gauze tightly into wound and press firmly. Do NOT pack neck wounds — gentle pressure only. Do not release pressure for any reason.
slides.warning: Torso and neck wounds require emergency surgery. Your only job is to maintain pressure until EMS arrives.
slides.tts_text: Maintain firm direct pressure. For groin or armpit wounds, pack gauze in tightly and hold firm. Never pack a neck wound.

slides.title: Treat for Shock
slides.content: Lay the person flat on their back. If no spine injury suspected, elevate legs about 12 inches. Keep them warm with a blanket or jacket. Do not give food or water. Speak calmly to keep them still.
slides.tts_text: Lay the person flat. Elevate their legs. Keep them warm. Do not give food or water.

slides.title: Continue Until EMS Arrives
slides.content: Maintain pressure without stopping. Monitor breathing and level of consciousness. If person stops breathing and has no pulse, begin CPR.

completion.message: Keep pressure on wound. Help is on the way.

slides.tts_text: Keep pressure on the wound. Monitor breathing. Emergency services are on the way.
